topic: Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters
our_argument_summary: Section 6.1 argues that the dome's headline claim of 'zero fitted parameters' is false because at least seven undisclosed parameters are fitted to observations rather than derived from dome geometry: B(r) coefficients (62,376 and 64,852 nT), coupling constant κ (1.67), aetheric damping ratio (26%), Finsler scaling function n(r), equatorial ring radius (14,105 km), and NMP deceleration constant (0.08). The section examines WIN-012, WIN-013, WIN-056 as examples of this pattern — each claims zero free parameters but uses values adopted from globe physics or fitted to observations.

holes_found.description: WIN-012 MISATTRIBUTION: Section 6.1 labels the B(r) = 62,376 × exp(−r/8619) + 64,852 × exp(−r/8619) formula as 'WIN-012 (Geomagnetic field)' but WIN-012 is actually about the mag-gravity coupling constant κ = 1.67 nT/μGal. The B(r) two-pole formula belongs to WIN-053 (Two-pole geomagnetic model). This is a factual misattribution that a dome defender would immediately spot and use to discredit the section's credibility. The B(r) argument itself is valid — the coefficients ARE fitted — but it's filed under the wrong WIN.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'WIN-012 (Geomagnetic field)' to 'WIN-053 (Two-pole geomagnetic model)' for the B(r) formula paragraph. Consider adding a separate WIN-012 paragraph about κ being empirically derived (which is already covered in the κ paragraph below).

holes_found.description: 62,376/64,852 COEFFICIENTS MAY NOT BE THE DOME'S CURRENT CLAIM: Per analyst globe-fingerprint analysis (WIN-012.json and WIN-053.json), the dome's model page uses B(r) = 60,000 × exp(−r/8619) (a round number), and the two-pole form with specific coefficients is listed as OPEN-011 (under development, not yet solved as of April 2026). The 62,376/64,852 values appear on the dome's wins.html for WIN-053 display but may represent a developmental/preliminary version. Our section attacks these specific coefficients as 'fitted parameters' but the dome could respond that the two-pole form is explicitly acknowledged as under development and the working single-pole form uses a round 60,000 nT — which is arguably also fitted, but our text doesn't address that version.
holes_found.recommendation: Acknowledge both forms: (a) the published single-pole B(r) = 60,000 × exp(−r/8619) where 60,000 is an undisclosed fitted constant, and (b) the developmental two-pole form (OPEN-011) where 62,376 and 64,852 are additional fitted constants. This makes the argument more robust — even the simpler version has a fitted amplitude coefficient.

holes_found.description: 26% AETHERIC DAMPING CALLED 'UNDISCLOSED' BUT IS OPENLY STATED: The dome openly reports the 26% gap between theoretical (10.59 Hz) and measured (7.83 Hz) Schumann frequency in its wins text (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 57). Our section lists this among 'undisclosed parameters' but it's not undisclosed — it's prominently featured. A dome defender would say 'we reported 26% openly as an observable gap; calling it undisclosed is dishonest.' The real issue is that the dome treats this observable residual as evidence for aetheric damping without independent verification — which is a weaker but more accurate criticism than calling it 'undisclosed.'
holes_found.recommendation: Change the framing from 'undisclosed parameter' to 'empirically observed ratio treated as a model confirmation rather than a free parameter.' The 26% is observable, but the CAUSE (aetheric medium) is the unjustified assumption. The dome openly states the value but doesn't acknowledge it as a degree of freedom.

holes_found.description: NMP DECELERATION CONSTANT (0.08) AND INITIAL RATE (55 km/yr) ARE OPENLY PUBLISHED: The dome's live power page explicitly states 'rate = 55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015)) km/yr' (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 64). These values aren't hidden — they're prominently displayed. The critique should be that they're fitted to NOAA NMP data (the formula matches observed drift rates by design) rather than derived from dome geometry. Calling them 'undisclosed' when they're on the live dashboard undermines our credibility.
holes_found.recommendation: Reframe: these parameters are openly stated but have no derivation from dome geometry. The values 55 and 0.08 are clearly curve-fitted to the observed NMP trajectory — a genuine 'zero parameter' prediction would derive these from disc radius, H(r), or aetheric properties.

holes_found.description: DOME DEFENDER'S STRONGEST COUNTER-ARGUMENT NOT PREEMPTED: A sophisticated dome defender would argue that λ_g = 8,619 km IS derived from dome geometry (it's the e-folding length of the firmament height function H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619)), and that the claim 'zero fitted parameters' refers specifically to λ_g being reused across domains without re-fitting — not to the absence of ALL numerical constants in ALL formulas. In other words, the dome's claim is narrower than we represent it: one scale length, derived from the firmament curve, appears in multiple unrelated formulas. Our section doesn't adequately address this narrower (and more defensible) version of the claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a paragraph addressing the dome's actual claim structure: even if we grant that λ_g appears across domains, the formulas using λ_g still require additional constants (amplitudes, coupling coefficients, damping ratios) that ARE fitted per-domain. The 'zero fitted parameters' claim should specify 'zero additional fitted parameters beyond λ_g' — which is demonstrably false, as each domain introduces domain-specific fitted constants.

holes_found.description: MISSING QUANTITATIVE COMPARISON: The section lists seven undisclosed parameters but doesn't compare this against the dome's explicit claim of 'zero.' A simple statement like 'we identify at least N parameters that are fitted to observations, compared to the claimed zero' would make the argument punchier. Currently the reader has to count the bullet points themselves.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a summary count at the end of the parameter list: 'In total, we identify at least seven parameters that are fitted to observations rather than derived from dome geometry — compared to the claimed zero.'

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's live power dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt) shows that predicted and observed values for κ are 1.67 and 1.677 respectively — but both values trace back to the SAME measurement event (BOU 2017 eclipse). The 'observed' value is the original measurement, and the 'predicted' value is that same measurement rounded. This is monitoring by tautology, not genuine prediction-vs-observation. Section 6.1 should cite this as a concrete example of how the 'zero parameter' claim operates in practice: the 'prediction' IS the observation, relabeled.

The dome claims 'all ECM constants locked before live data pulled' (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 160). But locking a constant that was originally fitted to observations doesn't make it a prediction — it makes it a post-hoc fit that is now treated as fixed. The distinction between 'fitted then locked' and 'derived then tested' is crucial and our section could make it more explicitly. A constant derived from geometry and THEN confirmed by data is impressive. A constant fitted to data and THEN monitored against the same data source is circular.

The H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619) function itself has TWO parameters: H_0 = 8537 km (amplitude) and λ_g = 8619 km (scale length). The dome presents λ_g as geometrically derived but H_0 = 8537 is itself chosen to approximately match the Schumann-derived firmament height (~9,572 km at the pole, per the dome's own calculation). The 12.1% discrepancy between H(0) = 8537 and the Schumann prediction of 9,572 km is itself evidence of fitting: H_0 was adjusted to split the difference between the Schumann estimate and other constraints. Our section mentions the Schumann inconsistency for WIN-056 but doesn't highlight the H_0 vs Schumann discrepancy as evidence of parameter fitting.

kernel_of_truth.description: There IS something genuinely interesting about a single scale length (λ_g = 8,619 km) appearing in the firmament height curve and then plausibly relating to geomagnetic field decay. In standard geophysics, the Earth's radius (~6,371 km) does serve as a natural scale for both gravitational and geomagnetic phenomena. The dome's λ_g ≈ 8,619 km could be a distorted reflection of this physical scale (it's roughly 1.35× Earth's radius), and the idea that cavity geometry determines multiple physical phenomena simultaneously is correct for actual resonant cavities. If the dome had shown a rigorous derivation from cavity dimensions → λ_g → predictions, AND those predictions preceded observations, the claim would have genuine scientific weight.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The problem is that the derivation chain runs backwards: observations → λ_g → 'prediction.' The firmament height function H(r) was fitted to reconcile three contradictory H measurements (raw-text/05-model.txt lines 17-20), meaning λ_g was CHOSEN to make those measurements consistent, not predicted beforehand. Once λ_g is determined by fitting, reusing it in other formulas is curve-fitting by relay — you're propagating a fitted value, not making an independent prediction. Additionally, each domain where λ_g appears requires additional domain-specific constants (amplitudes, coupling coefficients) that are also fitted, making 'zero fitted parameters' false regardless of λ_g's provenance.

text_fingerprint.note: Section 6.1 from part6 in sections.json. Contains 6 paragraphs covering B(r) coefficients, WGS84 matching, solar elevation, κ coupling, and summary of undisclosed parameters.

claims_checked: κ = 10.9/6.5 = 1.67 (verified: 1.6769, rounds to 1.68 not 1.67 — dome value is truncated)
NMP formula: rate = 55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015)) gives 55/36.9/24.7/16.6 km/yr for 2015/2020/2025/2030 (verified)
H(0) = 8537 km vs Schumann prediction 9572 km = 12.1% discrepancy (verified)
quantitative_verification.computations: All arithmetic in section 6.1 verified via Python. The B(r) simplification is mathematically correct. κ = 10.9/6.5 = 1.6769 (dome truncates to 1.67). Schumann height derivation matches dome's stated value. NMP deceleration formula produces realistic drift rates.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Minor: κ = 10.9/6.5 = 1.6769, which rounds to 1.68, not 1.67. The dome truncates rather than rounds. This is not materially wrong but could be noted as an additional red flag — the 'constant' isn't even precisely stated.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender would argue: 'The zero fitted parameters claim refers to the aetheric scale length λ_g = 8,619 km, which is geometrically derived from the firmament height curve H(r). This single constant then independently predicts phenomena across six domains without re-fitting. The amplitude coefficients (B field strengths, gravity offsets, etc.) are boundary conditions, not fitted parameters — just like standard physics uses Earth's mass as a boundary condition for gravity calculations without calling it a 'fitted parameter.' Your review conflates boundary conditions with curve fitting. When we say zero fitted parameters, we mean zero parameters adjusted after the predictions were registered — the constants were locked, the data came in, and they matched. That's the definition of prediction, not fitting.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Address the boundary-condition defense directly: in standard physics, 'boundary conditions' like Earth's mass have independent measurements and derivations from multiple sources. The dome's amplitude coefficients (62,376 nT, 1.67 nT/μGal, 26% damping) have no independent derivation — they're measured once and declared universal. More critically, the 'locked before data' claim is misleading because the constants were locked AFTER being fitted to historical data. Locking a fitted constant is not the same as deriving a constant from first principles.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Section 6.1 misattributes the B(r) formula to WIN-012 when it belongs to WIN-053. However, the underlying arguments across these WINs are consistent: all four are correctly identified as having fitted parameters despite the dome's 'zero parameter' claim. The verdicts (Self-Contradicted for WIN-012 and WIN-056, Misleading for WIN-013, Self-Contradicted for WIN-053) are appropriate and mutually consistent.

deeper_analysis: Section 6.1 makes a fundamentally correct and important argument: the dome's 'zero fitted parameters' claim is false. The dome has at minimum seven undisclosed or unacknowledged fitted parameters across its key formulas. However, the section's presentation has several vulnerabilities that a sophisticated defender could exploit: (1) the WIN-012/WIN-053 misattribution is an unforced error, (2) calling openly published values 'undisclosed' overstates the case, and (3) not addressing the dome's narrower claim about λ_g being reused without re-fitting leaves the strongest defense unpreempted. The recommended fixes would make this section considerably more bulletproof while keeping the same core argument. The section is currently effective at the surface level but could be demolished by a technically literate dome defender who reads the actual dome source material and notes the misattribution and 'undisclosed' vs 'openly published' discrepancies.

citations_unchecked: Section 6.1 contains no DOI citations or external references — it references only internal WIN numbers and dome page content. The absence of external citations is appropriate here since the section analyzes the dome's own claims rather than external science.
